description:
You are a database administrator who has received two yearly performance extracts in CSV format.  
    Both files already exist on the system:

    • /home/user/data/query_stats_2023.csv  
    • /home/user/data/query_stats_2024.csv  

    Each CSV uses comma-separated values with *exactly* this header line  
        query_id,db,timestamp,exec_time_ms,cpu_ms,io_reads  

    Goal: produce a concise optimisation report inside a **new** directory  
    /home/user/db_opt_reports (the directory must be created by you).

    The report consists of three artefacts that the automated grader will check:

    1) /home/user/db_opt_reports/summary.json  
       Required JSON layout (pay attention to key names, nesting and ordering – numbers must appear as JSON numbers, not strings):

       {
         "average_exec_time_ms": {
           "<db-name-1>": <floating-point-average>,
           "<db-name-2>": <floating-point-average>
         },
         "top_slowest_queries": [
           {
             "query_id": "<id>",
             "db": "<db>",
             "timestamp": "<ISO-8601-stamp>",
             "exec_time_ms": <integer>
           },
           ...
         ]
       }

       • average_exec_time_ms – one entry per distinct “db” value.  
       • top_slowest_queries – exactly the three rows (across BOTH CSV files) with the largest exec_time_ms, sorted in descending order of exec_time_ms.

    2) /home/user/db_opt_reports/top_slowest_queries.csv  
       Must contain the **same three** rows that appear in summary.json, comma-separated and in this column order (with header):

       query_id,db,timestamp,exec_time_ms

    3) /home/user/db_opt_reports/process.log  
       A five-line plain-text log capturing your workflow.  
       Each line must follow the strict pattern (square brackets included):

       [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] STEP: <STEP_NAME> - OK (<details>)

       Required STEP_NAME values *in this exact order*:  
          DOWNLOAD_DATA  
          MERGE_DATA  
          CALCULATE_AVG_EXEC_TIME  
          IDENTIFY_TOP_SLOWEST  
          WRITE_REPORTS  

       • The timestamp should be when you executed the step (any valid 24-hour local time is fine).  
       • <details> must be:  
         – for DOWNLOAD_DATA: “rows: 4+4” (reflecting the two source files)  
         – for MERGE_DATA: “rows: 8” (after concatenation)  
         – for CALCULATE_AVG_EXEC_TIME: “db1:4,db2:4”  
         – for IDENTIFY_TOP_SLOWEST: “rows: 3”  
         – for WRITE_REPORTS: simply leave <details> empty (i.e. “WRITE_REPORTS - OK”) or put “reports written” – grader only checks the first four steps for exact wording.

    Functional requirements you must meet (the grader verifies these through file inspection):

    • There are *exactly* 8 non-header data rows when both CSVs are merged.  
    • Duplicates (same query_id **and** timestamp) must be removed if they ever appear (they do not in the supplied files, but your script must handle the case).  
    • The average for each db is calculated over its own rows and must be written with standard floating-point representation (e.g. 103.75 not "103.75").  
    • The JSON and CSV outputs must be byte-for-byte identical apart from whitespace permitted inside JSON (indentation/spaces/newlines are optional).  
    • process.log must have five lines, one per step, in the required order and matching the exact STEP_NAME tokens above.

    Non-functional hints:

    • Use whatever command-line tools or scripting language you prefer (awk, csvkit, jq, python, etc.) – grader only cares about final file contents & log structure.  
    • All work must be done without sudo privileges.  
    • Keep intermediate files (if any) inside /home/user/db_opt_reports; they will be ignored by the grader.  
    • Remember to create /home/user/db_opt_reports before writing into it.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/data/query_stats_2023.csv  
       Contents (including header, 5 lines total):

       query_id,db,timestamp,exec_time_ms,cpu_ms,io_reads
       Q1,db1,2023-06-15T10:15:30,120,80,200
       Q2,db2,2023-07-20T11:30:00,250,150,300
       Q3,db1,2023-08-05T09:45:10,90,60,100
       Q4,db2,2023-09-12T14:20:05,310,200,400

    2. /home/user/data/query_stats_2024.csv  
       Contents (including header, 5 lines total):

       query_id,db,timestamp,exec_time_ms,cpu_ms,io_reads
       Q1,db1,2024-01-18T12:10:20,110,70,190
       Q5,db2,2024-02-25T15:40:00,340,220,450
       Q6,db1,2024-03-03T08:05:55,95,65,110
       Q7,db2,2024-04-10T16:25:30,280,180,350

    Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

    1. /home/user/db_opt_reports/summary.json  
       Canonical (minified) form used by grader:

       {"average_exec_time_ms":{"db1":103.75,"db2":295},"top_slowest_queries":[{"query_id":"Q5","db":"db2","timestamp":"2024-02-25T15:40:00","exec_time_ms":340},{"query_id":"Q4","db":"db2","timestamp":"2023-09-12T14:20:05","exec_time_ms":310},{"query_id":"Q7","db":"db2","timestamp":"2024-04-10T16:25:30","exec_time_ms":280}]}

       (Grader parses JSON so whitespace/indentation variations are tolerated.)

    2. /home/user/db_opt_reports/top_slowest_queries.csv  
       Exact CSV text (4 lines):

       query_id,db,timestamp,exec_time_ms
       Q5,db2,2024-02-25T15:40:00,340
       Q4,db2,2023-09-12T14:20:05,310
       Q7,db2,2024-04-10T16:25:30,280

    3. /home/user/db_opt_reports/process.log  
       The grader only verifies that:  
         • There are five lines.  
         • Line 1 contains “STEP: DOWNLOAD_DATA - OK (rows: 4+4)”  
         • Line 2 contains “STEP: MERGE_DATA - OK (rows: 8)”  
         • Line 3 contains “STEP: CALCULATE_AVG_EXEC_TIME - OK (db1:4,db2:4)”  
         • Line 4 contains “STEP: IDENTIFY_TOP_SLOWEST - OK (rows: 3)”  
         • Line 5 starts with “[” and contains “STEP: WRITE_REPORTS - OK” (detail string is ignored).  
       The grader ignores timestamps.

    Permissions: all initial files are readable by user. /home/user is writable so the agent can create /home/user/db_opt_reports with default user permissions.
